---
title: "Languages settings"
description: "Pick your venue's default and supported languages. Controls what diners can switch between."
sidebarTitle: "Languages"
icon: "globe"
---

Languages are a venue-level setting, not a per-menu one. You choose them
once in Settings, then every menu you publish can be translated into
those languages.

## Where to set it

In your workspace, go to **Settings → Languages**.

## Default language

The default language is what diners see when they haven't picked one
themselves, and what Rumizi falls back to when a translation is missing.

<Tip>
Pick the language most of your diners speak. If you're in Madrid, set the
default to Spanish even if you also support English.
</Tip>

## Supported languages

Tick every language you want to offer. Rumizi supports:

English, Spanish, Italian, Arabic, Hindi, Persian (Farsi), French,
Chinese, Punjabi, and Tagalog.

Right-to-left languages (Arabic, Persian) render RTL automatically —
you don't need to configure anything extra.

## What changes after saving

- The **language switcher** appears in the public menu header.
- The **Translations** tab appears in every menu editor.
- Tag names (like "Vegetarian") switch with the selected language too.

## Removing a language

Untick it in Settings. The translations you wrote aren't deleted — they
just stop being shown until you re-enable that language.
